clinical_coherence.notes: 38F with DM2, chronic sinusitis presenting with classic meningitis. Clinical picture is coherent but the HPI contains significant pronoun inconsistencies: patient is listed as female but HPI uses 'he/him/his' pronouns interchangeably with 'she/her'. The neuro exam states 'Obtunded' which is appropriate, and papilledema with bilateral Babinski signs adds severity. Hypotension (90/54) appropriately suggests sepsis physiology.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF WBC 3474 with 94% neutrophils, protein 353.5, glucose 11.4 -- classic bacterial meningitis. MRI with diffuse leptomeningeal enhancement and sulcal DWI restriction. Labs show leukocytosis with bandemia. All outputs perfectly concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G00.1 correct. Critical actions include empiric antibiotics, dexamethasone, blood cultures. The sepsis component with hypotension adds appropriate management complexity requiring fluid resuscitation.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic presentation with all cardinal features. Positive Gram stain if present, extreme CSF abnormalities. Correctly labeled straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 38F South Asian with DM2 and chronic sinusitis is plausible. The sinusitis predisposition and DM2 as risk factors are well-established.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case emphasizing sepsis with meningitis (hypotension), papilledema indicating raised ICP, and the importance of hemodynamic resuscitation alongside antibiotic therapy.

issues_found: HPI contains mixed pronoun usage -- patient sex is female but HPI alternates between he/him and she/her pronouns inconsistently, reducing clinical coherence
strengths: Sepsis physiology with hypotension adds realistic severity
Papilledema and bilateral Babinski signs reflect serious illness
